methods.description: Procedure for performing cross-pollination in Arabidopsis by removing anthers before pollen maturation and dusting immature stigmas with pollen.

steps.description: Remove anthers from flowers before pollen maturation (dehiscence).
steps.searchable_summary: remove anthers from flowers before pollen maturation

steps.description: Dust immature stigmas with pollen immediately or 2-3 days later (mature).

methods.description: Process for inducing mutations in Arabidopsis seeds using ethyl nitrosourea and screening for sterile mutations.

steps.description: Immerse seeds in 4 mM ethyl nitrosourea for 24 hr at room temperature.
steps.searchable_summary: immerse seeds in ethyl nitrosourea solution

steps.description: Grow mutagenized (M1) seeds and screen plants generated from self-seeds (M2) for sterile mutations at a density of ~500 seeds/ft².
steps.searchable_summary: grow and screen M1 and M2 seeds for sterile mutations

methods.description: Description of the growth conditions for Arabidopsis strains used in experiments.

steps.description: Maintain growth conditions at 24-hr fluorescent lighting [85 micro-Einsteins per square meter (~E/m²)] at 18-22°C and 50-70% relative humidity (ambient conditions) or 90% relative humidity (Conviron chamber).
steps.searchable_summary: set growth conditions for Arabidopsis strains
